---
title: "Unit 2 Task 1 Lesson"
format: html
---

```{python}
import polars as pl
import requests #new for reading in JSON
import io       #new for reading in JSON
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```

```{python}

url = "https://byuidatascience.github.io/DS250-Course-Polars/Data/house-price.json"

# Use the requests library to send an HTTP GET request to that URL.
# The result (response) contains the server’s reply, including the file contents.
# The actual bytes of the file are in response.content.
response = requests.get(url)

# response.content is raw bytes, not a file.
# io.BytesIO(...) wraps those bytes in a file-like object in memory, which Polars can read from.
# pl.read_json(...) parses the JSON and loads it into a Polars DataFrame called df.
houses = pl.read_json(io.BytesIO(response.content))
```


```{python}
houses.head()
```

We're going to gice a price bump of 5000 * (sqft / 1000) when unfurnished
semifurnished 2000 / 1000sqft
furnished no bump

```{python}
adjusted = houses.with_columns(
    adj_price = pl.when(pl.col("furnishingstatus") == "unfurnished")
    .then(pl.col('price') + 5000 * pl.col('area') / 1000)
    .when(pl.col('furnishingstatus') == 'semi-furnished')
    .then(pl.col('price') + 2000 * pl.col('area') / 1000)
    .otherwise(pl.col('price'))
)
adjusted.tail()
```



```{python}
(
    adjusted.select(
        pl.col(pl.Float64, pl.Int64)
    )
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col(['price', 'adj_price']) / 1000).name.suffix('_k')
        )
)
```


```{python}
adjusted.with_columns(
    (pl.col())
)
```


```{python}
import polars.selectors as cs
adjusted.with_columns((cs.contains('price') / 1000).name.suffix('_k'))
```


```{python}
import polars.selectors as cs 

adjusted.select(cs.numeric())
```


```{python}
df_dumb = adjusted.with_columns(pl.col('bedrooms').cast(pl.String))
df_dumb
undone = df_dumb.with_columns(pl.col('bedrooms').cast(pl.Int64))
undone
```


```{python}
(
    ggplot(data = undone, mapping=aes(x = 'bedrooms', y='area'))
    + geom_boxplot(outlier_alpha=0)
    + scale_x_discrete(limits=[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7])

)
```
